title: Seismicity in the upper plate of the Northern Chilean offshore forearc: Evidence of splay fault south of the Mejillones Peninsula
abstract: A 3D tomographic model of the upper plate was developed using arrival times from the 1995 CINCA seismic network aftershock catalog from the Antofagasta earthquake (Mw 8.1). Seismicity partially defined by a clear lineament between 22 and 5 km depth is interpreted as an active splay fault ~50 km long south of the Mejillones Peninsula. The splay fault zone shows decreased Vp and Vs velocities and increased Vp/Vs ratio suggesting fracturing and fluid saturation.

location.description: Northern Chilean offshore forearc south of Mejillones Peninsula (~23°S); splay fault imaging using CINCA seismic network

acquisition.vessel: R/V SONNE (SO104 CINCA95, 1995) — passive seismic network post-Antofagasta earthquake

data.name: CINCA95 OBH Passive Seismic Network — Antofagasta Aftershock Catalog ~23°S
data.description: Passive seismic recordings from the CINCA95 OBH+land network offshore Antofagasta (~23°S) used for 3D local earthquake tomography; defines splay fault ~50 km long south of Mejillones Peninsula

data.name: CINCA95 MCS Profiles — Antofagasta Region
data.description: MCS reflection profiles from CINCA95 (SO104) used as structural framework for interpreting the splay fault imaged by local earthquake tomography. [CINCA95 cruise (SO104, 1995) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

_data_note: CINCA95 OBH passive network. MCS data from SO104 accessible via MGDS. Passive OBH data may be at IRIS.

processing.summary: Aftershock P-wave arrival times from the CINCA95 seismic network (OBH + land stations offshore/onshore Antofagasta, 1995) were inverted for a 3D Vp, Vs and Vp/Vs model of the northern Chilean offshore forearc at ~23°S. A splay fault ~50 km long south of Mejillones Peninsula is identified as a velocity anomaly (reduced Vp/Vs) between 5–22 km depth. CINCA95 MCS profiles provide structural context.
workflow: 2. P- and S-wave arrival time picking from OBH/land stations
3. 3D simultaneous inversion for hypocenters and velocity structure
processing.notes: Passive OBH study combined with MCS interpretation from CINCA95

analysis_notes: Tectonophysics paper; behind paywall. CINCA95 MCS data at MGDS. Passive OBH data from 1995 may be at IRIS.
